skill.name: qwen-image
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Qwen-Image-2.0 (fast), Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro (professional text rendering). Capabilities: text-to-image, multi-image editing, complex text rendering. Triggers: qwen image, qwen-image, alibaba image, dashscope image, qwen image 2, qwen image pro
skill.summary: Generate and edit images using Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: tul-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected external commands and network patterns, but evaluation confirms these are legitimate uses of the inference.sh CLI for image generation. The backtick detections are false positives from markdown code fences. URLs point to official inference.sh service. No malicious behavior confirmed.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Commands
medium_findings.description: Skill invokes inference.sh CLI (infsh) for image generation. This is the documented, intended behavior for this skill type.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static analyzer detects backticks as shell execution patterns, but these are markdown code fences in documentation, not actual code execution. The skill legitimately calls infsh CLI as designed.

low_findings.title: External Network URLs
low_findings.description: Documentation URLs and API endpoints for inference.sh service. Required for skill functionality.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hardcoded URLs point to inference.sh official documentation and demo images. These are legitimate service endpoints required for the skill to function.

content.value_statement: Create stunning images from text prompts and edit existing images using Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models through a simple CLI interface.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using Qwen-Image-2.0 model
Create professional text-rendered images with Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro
Edit images using multi-reference input (up to 3 reference images)
Control output with custom resolution, seeds, and negative prompts
Generate multiple images (1-6) in a single request
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI (infsh) installation and account
Image resolution constrained to 512x512 to 2048x2048 pixels
Network connectivity required for API calls

use_cases.title: Create promotional graphics
use_cases.description: Generate text-heavy posters and marketing materials with precise text rendering using the Pro model

use_cases.title: Prototype visual concepts
use_cases.description: Quickly generate image concepts from text descriptions for brainstorming and iteration

use_cases.title: AI-powered image editing
use_cases.description: Edit existing images by combining elements from multiple reference images

prompt_templates.title: Basic image generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of {description of scene} using qwen-image
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a simple image from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Custom resolution image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a {width}x{height} image showing {description} using qwen-image-pro model
prompt_templates.scenario: Create an image with specific dimensions

prompt_templates.title: Image with negative prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an image of {subject} using qwen-image, avoid {unwanted elements} in the output
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate image while excluding certain elements

prompt_templates.title: Reproducible generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate {description} with seed {number} for reproducible results using qwen-image
prompt_templates.scenario: Create deterministic images for testing or consistency

output_examples.input: Generate a serene mountain landscape at sunset using qwen-image
output_examples.output: Returns an array of generated images with metadata including dimensions, format (PNG), and generation parameters used.

output_examples.input: Create a poster with 'Sale' text using qwen-image-pro with resolution 1024x1536
output_examples.output: Returns professionally rendered text in the generated poster image with specified dimensions.

best_practices: Use qwen-image-pro for text-rendered designs and set prompt_extend to false for precise control
Specify resolution explicitly (width and height) to match your use case requirements
Use negative prompts to avoid common artifacts like blurriness or oversaturation
anti_patterns: Do not assume unlimited API credits - inference.sh may have rate limits
Avoid very long prompts beyond 800 characters as they may be truncated
Do not skip checking the watermark parameter if you need unbranded output

faq.question: What is inference.sh and do I need an account?
faq.answer: Yes, inference.sh is the platform providing the Qwen-Image models. You need to install the CLI (infsh) and log in with your account to use this skill.

faq.question: What is the difference between qwen-image and qwen-image-pro?
faq.answer: Pro model offers professional text rendering, fine-grained realism, and enhanced semantic adherence. Use Pro for text-heavy designs.

faq.question: How do I control image dimensions?
faq.answer: Set width and height parameters in your request. Total pixels must be between 512x512 and 2048x2048.

faq.question: Can I generate multiple images at once?
faq.answer: Yes, use the num_images parameter to generate 1-6 images in a single request.

faq.question: How do I get reproducible results?
faq.answer: Set the seed parameter to a specific integer value (0-2147483647) to reproduce the same image.

faq.question: What is prompt_extend?
faq.answer: When true (default), the model rewrites your prompt for better results. Set to false for precise control over the output.
